title: CDX Security Extension
description: Schema for security extension files in a CDX document

signer.description: Advisory signer display information extending base person. The authoritative identity is the signing-certificate (x5c) subject, not these fields.

trustedTimestamp.description: RFC 3161 trusted timestamp over a signature (§3.6). The token is authoritative: its messageImprint MUST bind this signature record (the §3.6 imprint over protected||'.'||signature) and its TSA certificate chain MUST validate to the verifier's TSA trust store; the genTime inside the token is the trusted reference time T. authority/time are advisory display only — never authoritative.

token.description: RFC 3161 TimeStampToken (CMS SignedData), base64. The authoritative timestamp; its imprint binds this signature and its TSA chain anchors to the verifier's TSA trust store (§3.6). The imprint hash algorithm is carried inside the token, so it is not duplicated here.

authority.description: Advisory display URL of the timestamping authority; NOT authoritative (the token's validated TSA identity is)

time.description: Advisory display time; NOT authoritative (the validated token genTime T is). Present for display only

ltvData.description: Unsigned long-term-validation material for a signature (§7.5): certificates and revocation responses stapled AFTER signing and verified independently against the verifier's trust roots, dated by a validated signature-timestamp. Stripping it only weakens a verdict (fail-closed); forged material fails validation. Archive-timestamp renewal for algorithm aging is a subsequent version.

certificates.description: Stapled DER certificates (base64) — intermediates and OCSP-responder certs needed to build paths offline

revocationInfo.description: Stapled revocation responses, verified independently and dated by the validated timestamp genTime T (§7.5)

manifestProjection.description: Canonical projection of the security-relevant manifest declarations a scoped signature attests (see section 9.7). Constructed from manifest.json by the normative projector and serialized with JCS. The document ID is carried by scope.documentId and is not repeated here.

cdx.description: Specification version, mirrored from the manifest

state.description: Document lifecycle state, mirrored from the manifest

presentation.description: Presentation declaration: each presentation part's type, path and hash, plus the default-selection flag on the default entry. A `precise` entry binds a precise-layout file's hash, so a declared precise layout is attested by a manifest-covering signature (section 9.7).

extensions.description: Required-extension set: each extension's id, version and required flag, plus the config of a required extension

configFiles.description: Extension-config file references ({path, hash}) declared in the top-level academic/semantic/legal/collaboration config slots, projected verbatim and sorted by JCS with duplicate paths collapsed (section 9.7). Binds auxiliary files that sit outside the document hash (e.g. academic numbering, semantic bibliography/glossary) so their content is attested. Omitted when empty.

assets.description: Asset-index references ({path, hash}) — the index file of each category declared in manifest.assets, projected as {path: index, hash} and sorted by JCS with duplicate paths collapsed (section 9.7). Each index enumerates every asset's (and image variant's) hash, so hash-pinning the index transitively binds assets that sit outside the document hash — notably fonts (referenced by family name from the presentation) and image variants (selected by display size). Omitted when empty.

accessControl.description: Access-control policy reference ({path, hash}) projected from manifest.security.accessControl (section 9.9) — the one security-block reference bound into the projection, so a manifest-covering signature attests the access-control policy. Only {path, hash} is bound; advisory storage hints are dropped. Omitted when absent.

lineage.description: Lineage, bound verbatim from the manifest

signaturePolicy.description: Required-signer set, projected verbatim from manifest.signaturePolicy (§3.12). Present iff the manifest declares one; binds the signature set against stripping and downgrade.

signatureScope.description: Scoped signature: an explicit, signed declaration of what a signature covers — always the document ID, plus optionally the manifest projection (section 9.7) and/or precise-layout hashes (section 9.3). A frozen or published document's signatures MUST include manifest.

documentId.description: The signed, authoritative document ID; MUST equal the recomputed manifest.id (security extension section 9.4). The unsigned top-level documentId is an advisory copy of this value.

manifest.description: Signed projection of the security-relevant manifest declarations (section 9.7). Present iff this signature attests the manifest; REQUIRED for a frozen or published document.

layouts.description: Map of layout path to layout file hash for visual attestation

publicJwk.description: A public JWK for a WebAuthn credential key, converted from its COSE key: EC (P-256/P-384) or OKP (Ed25519).

webauthnAssertion.description: A WebAuthn/FIDO2 assertion carrying the signature (section 6). The document is bound via clientDataJSON.challenge = BASE64URL(SHA-256(JCS(scope))); trust is a verifier pin on publicKey (the self-certifying model of section 6). credentialId is advisory and is not the trust anchor.

algorithm.description: COSE signature algorithm of the assertion — the WebAuthn subset: ES256 (MUST support), ES384, EdDSA

credentialId.description: Advisory WebAuthn credential identifier (base64url); NOT the trust anchor

clientDataJSON.description: base64url-encoded clientDataJSON bytes — signed and parsed verbatim, never re-serialized

signature.description: base64url-encoded signature over authenticatorData || SHA-256(clientDataJSON) (ECDSA in ASN.1 DER)

publicKey.description: The credential's COSE public key as a JWK; the authoritative trust anchor is a verifier pin on this key (section 6)

signature.description: A signature entry attesting `scope`. Exactly one shape: a detached-JWS signature (section 3.3 — `protected` + `signature`, the X.509/keyId paths) or a WebAuthn assertion (section 6 — `webauthn`).

scope.description: What the signature covers (document id, manifest projection, layouts). For the JWS paths it is the detached payload JCS(scope); for WebAuthn it is bound via clientDataJSON.challenge = BASE64URL(SHA-256(JCS(scope))).

signer.description: Advisory signer display information; the authoritative identity is the signing-certificate subject or the resolved/pinned credential

protected.description: JWS path: base64url-encoded signed JWS protected header (carries alg, sigT, and either x5c + x5t#S256 or kid)

signature.description: JWS path: base64url-encoded JWS signature over BASE64URL(protected) + '.' + JCS(scope)

timestamp.description: JWS path: RFC 3161 trusted timestamp over this signature (section 3.6)

ltv.description: JWS path: unsigned long-term-validation material (stapled certificates and revocation responses) for this signature (section 7.5)

webauthn.description: WebAuthn path: a FIDO2 assertion carrying the signature (section 6)

documentId.description: The document content hash. This unsigned top-level copy is ADVISORY: the authoritative, signed binding is each signature's scope.documentId, which MUST equal the recomputed manifest.id (security extension section 9.4). A verifier MUST NOT treat this field as authenticated.

signatures.description: Array of signatures (at least one — an empty set is a document that claims to be signed but is not)

p2s.description: PBES2 salt input (base64url), REQUIRED when keyManagement is PBES2-HS256+A256KW (RFC 7518 section 4.8). At least 8 octets (11 base64url chars) and unique per document; without it, offline password cracking can be precomputed across documents.

p2c.description: PBES2 iteration count, REQUIRED when keyManagement is PBES2-HS256+A256KW. MUST be at least 600000 (OWASP PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 guidance) and SHOULD be raised over time; a low count makes brute-force cheap.

encryptedPart.description: One encrypted part: its ciphertext file plus the AEAD iv and authentication tag. The iv MUST be unique per encryption under the content-encryption key (Security Extension README section 4.6); reusing an (key, iv) pair is a catastrophic AEAD misuse that breaks both confidentiality and integrity.

iv.description: Base64-encoded AEAD nonce / initialization vector, unique per (key, encryption)

path.description: Path to the encrypted file (typically with a .enc suffix)

encryptedContent.description: The encrypted parts — one per encrypted file (the content file, and optionally the Dublin Core metadata, README section 4.4). Each part MUST carry its own unique iv (README section 4.6); the schema does not enforce iv uniqueness.

recipients.description: Array of recipients who can decrypt

accessControlFile.description: Schema for a security/access-control.json file: a versioned wrapper around an accessControl policy. Referenced from manifest.security.accessControl as a {path, hash}; a manifest-covering signature attests that hash through the manifest projection (security extension section 9.9), so the policy cannot be swapped while a signature verifies. The policy is advisory authorization guidance — CDX binds its integrity, not its enforcement; a consumer applies it at its own discretion.
